title.explorer: A Book That Started a Revolution!

summary.scholar: Published in January 1776, Thomas Paine's pamphlet Common Sense made a powerful case for American independence in plain, accessible language. It sold approximately 500,000 copies — in a nation of 2.5 million — making it the best-selling publication in American history relative to population. Paine argued that hereditary monarchy was absurd and that America should seize the moment to create a republic.
summary.explorer: In January 1776, Thomas Paine wrote a little booklet called Common Sense that changed everything! He explained in simple words why America should break free from Britain. Almost everyone in America read it — it was like the most viral post ever! It convinced tons of people that independence was the right thing to do.

significance.scholar: Common Sense transformed public opinion from seeking reconciliation with Britain to demanding independence, directly influencing the Declaration of Independence six months later.
significance.explorer: Common Sense convinced regular Americans that independence wasn't crazy — it was common sense! Without this book, the Declaration of Independence might never have happened.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
